title: 7 Herbal Teas For Gum Infection
intro_desc: Gum chewers often suffer from gum infections due to the constant irritation and bacteria buildup in their mouths. The constant influx of sugar and other substances from the gum can lead to inflammation and infection, causing pain, swelling, and discomfort. This can make everyday activities like eating, talking, and even sleeping difficult. The pain can be severe, and in some cases, it can even lead to tooth loss if left untreated. People who chew gum regularly may experience bad breath, tender gums, and even bleeding gums. This can be embarrassing and affect their self-confidence. To make matters worse, the infection can spread to other parts of the mouth, causing more problems.  When gum chewers have a gum infection, they often try various treatments to alleviate the pain and discomfort. However, many of these treatments can have side effects or be expensive. This is where herbal teas come in. These teas are made from plants that have been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including gum infections. They work by reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and soothing the gums. By drinking these teas regularly, gum chewers can help treat their gum infections naturally and without the harsh chemicals found in some medications.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent gum infections by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria. Certain herbs like tea tree oil and echinacea have antibacterial properties that can fight off infections. Herbal teas like peppermint and chamomile can also calm gums and reduce swelling, creating a healthy environment for your teeth and gums.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent gum infection from forming. Certain teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have antibacterial properties that can reduce bacteria in the mouth. This can help prevent infections like gingivitis. Some herbs, like sage, may even help reduce inflammation in the gums, keeping them healthy.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a gentle way to help with gum infections, but it's not recommended to drink them every day. Some herbal teas, like those with eucalyptus or peppermint, may irritate your gums or mouth if consumed excessively. It's best to drink them in moderation or as directed on the label.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with gum infection, but the time it takes to show results varies. Some people notice improvement within a week, while others may take a few weeks. The effectiveness depends on the severity of the infection and the type of tea being used. Tea with ingredients like eucalyptus or peppermint can reduce inflammation and ease pain.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for a gum infection is most helpful in the morning or early afternoon. This is when your body is most active, and the tea can help boost your immune system and fight off the infection. The antibacterial properties in the tea can work effectively during this time to reduce inflammation and soothe the affected area.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas may interact with prescription medications for gum infection. For example, goldenseal and echinacea can slow down blood clotting, which might be a problem if you're taking blood thinners. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can also affect the way your body absorbs medications, including antibiotics.
faq_pregnant: It's generally a good idea to avoid herbal teas when you're pregnant, especially if you have a gum infection. Some herbal teas can affect your baby's growth or even cause complications. If you have a gum infection, your healthcare provider may prescribe medication to help clear it up.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for gum infection might sound like a good idea, but it's best to be cautious while breastfeeding. Some herbs can pass into your milk and affect your baby. For example, sage and peppermint tea might be safe in small amounts, but more research is needed to confirm their safety.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas contains compounds like eucalyptol, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and fight bacteria that cause gum infections. The tea also contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the gums from damage. When used as a mouthwash or gargle, Eucalyptus globulus tea can help loosen and remove plaque, a sticky film that forms on teeth and can lead to gum infections. To use Eucalyptus globulus tea for gum infection, make a tea by steeping one teaspoon of dried leaves in a cup of hot water for 5-7 minutes, then let it cool. Swish the tea around in your mouth for 30 seconds to 1 minute, then spit it out. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day. After using Eucalyptus globulus tea, you may notice a reduction in gum inflammation and pain, and your gums may look healthier. However, if your gum infection is severe, you should still consult a dentist for proper treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for gum infection

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains compounds like alkylamides, glycosides, and polyphenols, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat gum infection. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea may reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause gum disease. If you're experiencing a gum infection, try drinking Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals, to help soothe and calm the infected area. You can also rinse your mouth with the tea or apply it as a mouthwash to get the benefits directly on the affected area. However, it's essential to note that if you have a severe gum infection, you should consult your dentist or healthcare provider for proper treatment. Taking Echinacea purpurea tea may lead to reduced swelling and pain, and possibly even a decrease in the severity of the infection, allowing the gum tissue to heal faster.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for gum infection

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis teas contains compounds like tannins, flavonoids, and rosmarinic acid that help combat gum infections. These compounds have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and fight off the bacteria causing the infection. The tea also contains antioxidants that can help repair damaged gum tissue and promote healing. To use Salvia officinalis teas for gum infection, you can steep a teaspoon of dried sage leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Then, strain the tea and let it cool before rinsing your mouth with it. You can also use a mouthwash made from the tea to help kill bacteria and reduce inflammation. When you start using Salvia officinalis teas for gum infection, you may notice a reduction in swelling and pain within a few days. Your gums may start to heal, and the infection may clear up within 1-2 weeks. However, it's essential to continue using the tea as directed and to also practice good oral hygiene, such as brushing and flossing regularly, to prevent the infection from coming back.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal teas for gum infection

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat gum infection. The tea's main constituents, glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the mouth. These properties make it an effective remedy for gum infection, particularly for those with gingivitis or periodontitis.  To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for gum infection, start by rinsing your mouth with the tea several times a day. You can do this by steeping a teaspoon of dried licorice root in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then straining and cooling the tea before swishing it around your mouth for 30 seconds to 1 minute before spitting it out. Additionally, you can add the tea to your toothpaste or mouthwash to further reduce bacterial growth.  When using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for gum infection, you can expect to see improvements in your gum health within a few weeks. Your gums may become less inflamed, and the bleeding may decrease. The tea may also help to freshen your breath and reduce plaque buildup. However, it's essential to note that while Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can help alleviate symptoms, it may not completely cure gum infection. If your condition persists or worsens, you should consult a dentist or healthcare professional for further guidance.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for gum infection

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds can help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that cause gum infections. When you drink Zingiber officinale tea, the active ingredients can reach the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort associated with gum infections.  To use Zingiber officinale tea to help with gum infection, try the following: 1. Make a tea by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of fresh ginger in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. 2. Strain the liquid and let it cool down before using it as a mouthwash or drinking it as a warm tea. 3. Use the tea as a mouthwash 2-3 times a day for 7-10 days to help reduce inflammation and fight off infection. When you use Zingiber officinale tea as a treatment for gum infection, you may notice some improvements within a few days. The tea can help reduce swelling, ease pain, and make your gums feel cleaner. However, it's essential to note that Zingiber officinale tea is not a substitute for regular dental care. You should still practice good oral hygiene and visit your dentist regularly to prevent and treat gum infections effectively.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for gum infection

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which are known for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to combat the bacteria that cause gum infections, such as gingivitis and periodontitis. Thymol, in particular, has been shown to disrupt the cell membranes of bacteria, ultimately killing them. Carvacrol has also been found to reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of plaque, which is a major contributor to gum disease. To use Thymus vulgaris teas for gum infection, start by making a tea by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried thyme leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes. Let it cool down and then swish the liquid in your mouth for 30 seconds to 1 minute before spitting it out. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day. You can also add the tea to your toothpaste or mouthwash for extra protection. As you use Thymus vulgaris teas, you may start to notice a reduction in swelling and redness of your gums. Your breath may also improve, and you may experience a decrease in bleeding when brushing or flossing your teeth. In some cases, the infection may completely clear up, and the gums may become healthy and pink again. However, it's essential to note that gum infections can be severe, and if you experience persistent pain, bleeding, or swelling, you should consult a dentist or healthcare professional for proper evaluation and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for gum infection

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas contains compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help combat gum infection by reducing swelling, fighting off bacteria, and soothing pain. When taken as a tea, the menthol in Mentha x piperita helps to numb the gums, reducing sensitivity and discomfort.  To use Mentha x piperita teas for gum infection, you can brew a cup by steeping a teaspoon of dried mint leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and let it cool before rinsing your mouth with it. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for best results. You can also mix a few drops of peppermint oil with water to create a mouthwash, but make sure to dilute it properly to avoid irritation.  As you start using Mentha x piperita teas, you may notice a reduction in swelling and redness in the affected area. The tea may also help to ease pain and discomfort, making it easier to eat and speak. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties may help to prevent further infections, promoting healing and recovery. However, it's essential to note that if your gum infection persists or worsens, you should consult a dentist or healthcare professional for proper evaluation and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for gum infection
